content.text:
I understand. I'm ready to explore the codebase and design implementation plans in read-only mode. I'll use Glob, Grep, Read, and read-only Bash commands to understand the project structure and architecture.

Let me start by exploring the current state of this repository:

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">find /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC -type f -name "*.ts" -o -name "*.tsx" -o -name "*.js" -o -name "*.jsx" -o -name "*.json" -o -name "*.md" 2>/dev/null | head -50</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
The user just said "Warmup" which seems like they're just testing that I'm ready. Let me explore the codebase to understand what we're working with.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Glob">
<parameter name="pattern">**/*</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
Let me see what files exist in this repository.
</thinking>

<function_calls>
<invoke name="Bash">
<parameter name="command">ls -la /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC/</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<thinking>
The repository appears to be freshly initialized with just a single "init" commit. Let me see what's there.
</thinking>

Based on my exploration, this appears to be a freshly initialized Git repository with minimal content (just the initial commit "init"). The repository is currently clean with no modified files.

I'm ready to receive requirements and design implementation plans. Please share:
1. The requirements or feature you'd like me to plan
2. Any specific perspective you'd like me to apply to the design process
3. Any existing files or patterns I should examine
